theory.canonical_statement: A Trawinistic manifold is a smooth manifold $(M,g)$ equipped with a Lorentzian metric $g$ such that $\det g|_p = 0$ at the TZP and $g$ is non-degenerate elsewhere.
theory.technical_interpretation: For any closed loop $\GammaLoop$ encircling the TZP in $\Manifold$, there exists a homotopy that deforms $\GammaLoop$ into a lemniscate (figure-eight) whose self-intersection occurs at $p$.

canonical_equation.source_section_latex:
\[
(M,g)\ \text{is Trawinistic}
\quad \Longleftrightarrow \quad
\det g\vert_{p}=0
\ \text{and}\ 
\det g(x)\neq 0 \ \text{for}\ x\neq p
\]
\[
g^{-1}(x)\ \text{exists for}\ x\neq p,
\qquad
g^{-1}\vert_{p}\ \text{does not exist}
\]

equation_progression.interpretive_role: This identifies the exact geometric location where ordinary metric structure breaks and the Trawinistic manifold departs from standard relativity.
This preserves usable differential geometry everywhere outside the zero point, so the singularity is localized rather than diffuse.

formula_latex_blocks:
g^{-1}\vert_{p}\ \text{does not exist},
\qquad
g^{-1}(x)\ \text{exists for}\ x\neq p
equation_progression.interpretive_role: This completes the progression by separating the normalized exterior geometry from the singular anchor that all later constructions reference.

external_sources.title: Trawinistic Manifold
external_sources.canonical_claim: A Trawinistic manifold is a smooth manifold $(M,g)$ equipped with a Lorentzian metric $g$ such that $\det g|_p = 0$ at the TZP and $g$ is non-degenerate elsewhere.
external_sources.exact_language: This source (Pseudoinversion of Degenerate Metrics) supports the mathematical structure (degenerate metric, pseudoinverse) by showing that This paper examines differential operators on manifolds equipped with degenerate metrics.
external_sources.remaining_gap: While the cited source provides partial support, a dedicated mathematical or physical proof specific to the Trawinistic framework is still required to fully establish this entry.
external_sources.relevance: This paper examines differential operators on manifolds equipped with degenerate metrics. It notes that many geometric operators are defined via the dual metric g* (the inverse of the metric), but when the metric is degenerate the inverse does not exist. The authors study pseudoinversion techniques to define gradients and Laplacians in this context.

promotion_ledger.note: Append-only proof-support promotion; final physical certificate still requires review of cited source fit.

proof_pipeline.scope_note: This metadata records authorship, lineage, and proof-pipeline artifact links. It does not by itself assert that the entry has a completed native analytic proof.
